name: Cannoli
instructions:
1. Mix the Flour, some of the White Sugar, and Cinnamon together in a bowl. Cut in the Shortening until the mixture becomes crumbly. Add the White Wine, Water, White Vinegar, whole Egg, and Egg Yolk. Mix until the dough becomes stiff, then knead vigorously for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Wrap the dough tightly and refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours to rest.

2. Divide the chilled dough into 3 equal parts. Flatten each piece and roll it through a pasta machine to the thinnest setting, creating very thin sheets. Cut the sheets into circles and carefully wrap each circle around cannoli tubes. Seal the edge with beaten Egg White to prevent them from opening during frying.

3. Heat the Sunflower Oil in a deep pot to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C). Carefully fry the shells while still on the tubes until they turn golden brown and crispy, 2 to 3 minutes. Remove from oil, let cool slightly, then carefully slide the shells off the tubes. Repeat the process for all remaining dough.

4. Mix the Ricotta and remaining White Sugar in a bowl until smooth and creamy. Gently fold in the chopped Dark Chocolate and Lemon Zest until evenly distributed. Using a piping bag or spoon, fill the cooled shells with the ricotta mixture from both ends. Dust with additional sugar before serving for a beautiful finish.
simpleInstructions:
1. Mix dough ingredients with the liquids, knead for 10 minutes, and refrigerate 1-2 hours.

2. Roll out dough very thin, cut into circles, wrap around cannoli tubes, seal with Egg White, and fry until golden.

3. Mix Ricotta filling with White Sugar, Dark Chocolate, and Lemon Zest. Fill cooled shells and dust with sugar.
